steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Both terms share the factor \(x^2\). Subtract the coefficients: \(13x^2 - 7x^2 = (13 - 7)x^2 = 6x^2\).

steps.title: Eliminate choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '\(-91x^2\)': \(-91\) would come from multiplying \(13 \times (-7)\), not subtracting. ✗
  • Choice B — '\(6x^2\)': \(13 - 7 = 6\), so this is correct. ✓
  • Choice C — '\(20x^2\)': \(20\) would come from adding \(13 + 7\), not subtracting. ✗
  • Choice D — '\(40x^2\)': No arithmetic operation on 13 and 7 yields 40. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
